title: VMware NSX Transport Node Data Plane Interface Errors and Drops (Per-Physical-NIC vmnic Counters, FCS/CRC, GENEVE MTU Pressure, esxcli NIC Stats, nsxdp-cli Queue Telemetry)
exclusions: VMware NSX Edge north-south throughput, BGP peers, Tier-0 SR dataplane `DPDK` counters, native load balancer pools (UC-18.2.4); GENEVE TEP tunnel-state / BFD adjacency telemetry without per-`vmnic` physics (UC-18.2.5); Policy logical segments / replication modes / MAC realization drift (UC-18.2.3); Distributed Firewall verdict streams (`vmware:nsxt:firewall`) inside `vsip` (UC-18.2.1); Tier-0/Tier-1 NAT rule pools (UC-18.2.11); NSX Manager cluster quorum / Corfu / VIP narratives (UC-18.2.13); NSX Intelligence OLAP leaderboards / anomaly scoring (UC-18.2.14); standalone vSphere estates without NSX transport-node overlays.
dataSources: `index=vmware` / `index=vmware_nsx` / `index=network_ops` combining **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) **`vmware:nsxt:transport_node`** snapshots sourced from **`GET /api/v1/transport-nodes`** Policy or Manager REST pulls plus nested NIC inventories where exported; **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase 3215](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3215)) forwarding **`vmware:vc:event`** Host alarms (**`alarm.NetworkConnectivityLostAlarm`**) plus **`vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** joins; scripted **`inputs.conf`** probes emitting **`esxi:nic:stats`** (`esxcli network nic stats get -n <vmnic>` plus **`nsxdp-cli netcpa stats`** **`vsip`** counters such as **`nsxt-vlan-overlay-pnic-rx-drop`**, **`nsxt-overlay-tep-encap-error`**, **`nsxt-bfd-pnic-error`**); **`vmware:esxi:syslog`** (`vmkernel.log`) lines referencing NIC resets; optional **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** Manager anecdotes plus VMware **Aria Operations** JSON forwarded via syslog/HEC.
app: **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) covering **`vmware:nsxt:transport_node`** / **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** parsing; **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase 3215](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3215)) for **`vcenter`** connectivity alarms and inventory joins; optional **`Splunk Connect for Syslog`** receivers scaling scripted **`esxi:nic:stats`** ingest.
spl:
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_ops)
(sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:transport_node" OR sourcetype="esxi:nic:stats")
earliest=-24h@m
| eval tn_key=trim(coalesce(transport_node_id,transport_node,tn_uuid,entity_id,host,hostname,management_ip,""))
| eval nic_key=lower(trim(coalesce(vmnic,pnic,pNic,network_adapter,nic_name,if_name,"")))
| eval rx_errors_num=tonumber(coalesce(rx_errors,rx_errors_total,"0"))
| eval tx_errors_num=tonumber(coalesce(tx_errors,tx_errors_total,"0"))
| eval rx_drops_num=tonumber(coalesce(rx_drops,rx_drop_count,in_discards,"0"))
| eval tx_drops_num=tonumber(coalesce(tx_drops,tx_drop_count,out_discards,"0"))
| eval fcs_errors_num=tonumber(coalesce(fcs_errors,fcs_err,bad_fcs,"0"))
| eval crc_errors_num=tonumber(coalesce(crc_errors,crc_err,bad_crc_count,"0"))
| eval alignment_errors_num=tonumber(coalesce(alignment_errors,in_errors_align,"0"))
| eval pause_frames_rx_num=tonumber(coalesce(pause_frames_rx,rx_pause_frames,"0"))
| eval tail_drop_num=tonumber(coalesce(tail_drop_count,rx_tail_drop_count,"0"))
| eval nobuffer_num=tonumber(coalesce(noBuffer_count,no_buffer_errors,"0"))
| eval nsxt_vlan_overlay_rx_drop=tonumber(coalesce(nsxt_vlan_overlay_pnic_rx_drop,"0"))
| eval nsxt_vlan_overlay_tx_drop=tonumber(coalesce(nsxt_vlan_overlay_pnic_tx_drop,"0"))
| eval nsxt_overlay_encap_err=tonumber(coalesce(nsxt_overlay_tep_encap_error,"0"))
| eval nsxt_bfd_pnic_err=tonumber(coalesce(nsxt_bfd_pnic_error,"0"))
| eval dp_overlay_fault=nsxt_vlan_overlay_rx_drop+nsxt_vlan_overlay_tx_drop+nsxt_overlay_encap_err+nsxt_bfd_pnic_err
| eval phy_fault=fcs_errors_num+crc_errors_num+alignment_errors_num
| eval composite_fault=rx_errors_num+tx_errors_num+phy_fault+tail_drop_num+nobuffer_num
| eval overlay_pressure=rx_drops_num+tx_drops_num
| where composite_fault>0 OR overlay_pressure>50 OR pause_frames_rx_num>2500 OR dp_overlay_fault>0
| bin _time span=15m
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
    sum(rx_errors_num) as sum_rx_errors,
    sum(tx_errors_num) as sum_tx_errors,
    sum(rx_drops_num) as sum_rx_drops,
    sum(tx_drops_num) as sum_tx_drops,
    sum(composite_fault) as sum_composite_fault,
    max(fcs_errors_num) as peak_fcs,
    max(crc_errors_num) as peak_crc,
    max(alignment_errors_num) as peak_align,
    max(pause_frames_rx_num) as peak_pause_rx,
    max(tail_drop_num) as peak_tail_drop,
    max(dp_overlay_fault) as peak_dp_overlay_fault,
    max(nsxt_overlay_encap_err) as peak_encap_err
  by _time tn_key nic_key
| sort 0 tn_key nic_key _time
| streamstats window=4 global=f avg(sum_rx_errors) as prev_avg_rx_err by tn_key nic_key
| eval rx_jump=sum_rx_errors-prev_avg_rx_err
| eval overlay_pressure_sum=sum_rx_drops+sum_tx_drops
| eval severity_mark=case(
    peak_fcs>0 OR peak_crc>0 OR peak_align>0, "physical_crc_fcs_align",
    peak_pause_rx>10000, "pause_upstream_pressure",
    peak_tail_drop>100 OR rx_jump>75, "ring_tail_drop_burst",
    peak_encap_err>0 OR peak_dp_overlay_fault>0, "geneve_overlay_dp_counters",
    overlay_pressure_sum>500 AND sum_composite_fault=0, "drops_without_incremental_phy_errors",
    1=1, "general_fault_watch")
| where severity_mark!="general_fault_watch" OR rx_jump>50 OR overlay_pressure_sum>200 OR peak_pause_rx>5000
| sort -last_seen
| head 400
| table last_seen tn_key nic_key severity_mark peak_fcs peak_crc peak_align peak_pause_rx peak_tail_drop peak_encap_err peak_dp_overlay_fault sum_rx_errors sum_tx_errors sum_rx_drops sum_tx_drops rx_jump sum_composite_fault overlay_pressure_sum
description: Physical NIC faults on VMware ESXi transport nodes chew overlay reliability before routing tables blink: incremental **FCS** and **CRC** tallies signal scratched optics or brittle copper runs on **TEP**-bearing uplinks; **alignment** bursts hint at duplex-speed mismatches against **ToR** ports; rising **pause-frame** receipts mean upstream switches asked your host to brake while queues refill; **tail-drop** counters tied to **vsip** watchdog telemetry expose kernel rings starved despite calm CPU dashboards. GENEVE wraps roughly fifty bytes outside inner Ethernet payloads, so a **1500-byte** host MTU quietly fragments overlay packets unless underlay **MTU** spans roughly **1700–1800** bytes across switches and NICs. Those fragments surface as **TX drop** spikes that teams mistake for firewall drift when they only stare at **Distributed Firewall** verdict charts. Splunk stitches **`vmnic`** counters from **`esxcli network nic stats`**, **`nsxdp-cli netcpa`** overlays (`nsxt-overlay-tep-encap-error`, **`nsxt-vlan-overlay-pnic-rx-drop`** families), Manager **`transport-node`** REST snapshots, and scripted **`esxi:nic:stats`** pulls so intermittent VM resets correlate with rack-facing interfaces rather than opaque overlay anecdotes.
value: Splunk charts separate hypervisor NIC faults from logical segments, Edge gateways, tunnel-state churn, NAT pools, Manager quorum, or Intelligence rankings because those sibling scopes intentionally omit **`vmnic`** physics. Faster triage assigns scratched cables versus upstream congestion versus tuning gaps: pairing **`alarm.NetworkConnectivityLostAlarm`** narratives with NIC matrices removes noisy escalation loops across virtualization, networking, and cabling crews; correlating **`transport_node`** identifiers against rack elevation avoids blaming applications when only one host uplink degrades; documenting GENEVE **MTU** posture beside counter bursts trims finger-pointing between VMware admins and switching teams; archiving **`tail_drop_count`** and **`noBuffer_count`** timelines proves buffer starvation hypotheses worth **`esxcli network nic ring current set`** adjustments during steady-state bursts.
implementation: Deploy **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** plus **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** collectors; enable scripted **`esxi:nic:stats`** probes wrapping **`esxcli network nic stats`** and **`nsxdp-cli netcpa stats`; normalize **`FIELDALIAS`** chains for **`vmnic`** synonyms; schedule **`macros/nsxt_pnic_fault`** saved searches every fifteen minutes across trailing twenty-four hour windows with **`transport_node`**/`**vmnic`** throttles.
visualization: (1) Heat-map: **`rack_id`** versus **`peak_crc`**/**`peak_fcs`** per **`vmnic`**. (2) Matrix: **`transport_node`** × **`nic_key`** coloring **`tail_drop_num`** versus **`pause_frames_rx`**. (3) Leader-board: top ten **`vmnic`** tuples by **`sum_composite_fault`** density. (4) Rolling twenty-four hour **`streamstats`** panel tracking **`rx_jump`** bursts ahead of **`carrier`** alarms.
schema: CIM: Performance + Network_Traffic overlays for physical NIC counters
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate **Performance** once **`FIELDALIAS`** maps **`vmnic`**-level **`rx_errors`**, **`tx_errors`**, **`rx_drops`**, **`tx_drops`** into generic performance metrics keyed by **`host`**/**`device`**; accelerate **Network_Traffic** when **`bytes_in`**, **`bytes_out`**, **`packets_in`**, **`packets_out`** aliases exist for scripted feeds.

references.title: VMware NSX 4.2 Administration Guide (Transport Node context)
VMware KB — Verify ESXi NIC statistics and duplex settings

knownFalsePositives:
**`cable_replacement_window` / `sfp_reseat_event`.** Lift-work on optics spikes **`FCS`** briefly while strands settle; Splunk mirrors **`crc_errors`** bursts that vanish once modules seat. Tie paging holds to **`CHG`** tickets referencing those drills instead of blaming routing alone without leaf corroboration.

**`auto_negotiation_renegotiation` after reload.** **`IOS`**/**`EOS`** refreshes restart speed negotiations between **`vmnic`** uplinks and **`ToR`** ports so **`alignment_errors`** mimic frayed cabling though **`carrier`** stayed up. Suppress against **`CHG`** rows citing leaf reloads; verify duplex via **`esxcli network nic get`**.

**`pause_frame_storm`.** Elevated **`pause_frames_rx`** reflects congested switches issuing **`802.3x`** **`pause-frame`** requests while local **`vmnic`** silicon stays healthy. Compare **`pause`** ratios against **`peak_tail_drop`** before blaming NIC firmware.

**`vmnic_driver_upgrade` versus `ring_buffer_tuning_lowpower`.** Driver installs (`esxcli software vib install`) reset counters while collectors replay stale highs versus fresh zeros; record TA epochs before paging. Low-power blades shrink **`RX`**/**`TX`** **`ring`** depth toward **`256`**, raising expected **`tail_drop_count`** during bursts.

**`psirt_known_firmware_bug` (`broadcom_57414`, `mellanox_connectx_6`).** Vendor **`PSIRT`** advisories describe **`CRC`**/**`FCS`** patterns resembling overlay loss yet rooted in firmware; reconcile NIC serials with **`KB`** notes before optics RMA when microcode fixes apply.
detailedImplementation:
**Prerequisites**
• Install **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) on Search Heads plus Heavy Forwarders so **`vmware:nsxt:transport_node`** HTTPS feeds mirror **`GET /api/v1/transport-nodes`** inventories with durable **`transport_node_id`** anchors.
• Layer **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase 3215](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3215)) for **`vmware:vc:event`** alarms (**`alarm.NetworkConnectivityLostAlarm`**) and **`vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** joins whenever dual-uplink transports narrate cluster faults.
• Deploy scripted probes (`inputs.conf`) invoking **`esxcli network nic stats get -n vmnicX`** plus **`nsxdp-cli netcpa stats`** every 120–300 seconds into **`esxi:nic:stats`**, emitting JSON keys for rx_errors, tx_errors, rx_drops, tx_drops, fcs_errors, crc_errors, alignment_errors, pause_frames_rx, tail_drop_count, noBuffer_count, nsxt_vlan_overlay_pnic_rx_drop, nsxt_vlan_overlay_pnic_tx_drop, nsxt_overlay_tep_encap_error, nsxt_bfd_pnic_error aligned with **N-VDS** (**NSX-T 3.x**) versus **VDS** (**NSX 4.x**) teaming (**Loadbalance Source MAC**, **Failover Order**).
• Optional VMware **Aria Operations** JSON or **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** Manager tails plus **`vmware:esxi:syslog`** `vmkernel.log` excerpts describing NIC resets for Splunk parity with adapter dashboards.
• Limit collectors to **`Auditor`** plus tightly scoped **`Enterprise Administrator`** privileges; authenticate **`POST /api/session/create`** cookies toward **`GET /api/v1/transport-nodes`** responses without unlocking destructive Edge **`DPDK`** verbs monitored under **`UC-18.2.4`**.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
(1) Enable **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** modular inputs referencing **`GET /api/v1/transport-nodes`** JSON (`display_name`, `node_deployment_info`, `network_interfaces` when exported) landing as **`vmware:nsxt:transport_node`**.
(2) Aim **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** collectors at **`vcenter`** alarms so **`alarm.NetworkConnectivityLostAlarm`** narratives precede scripted **`esxi:nic:stats`** spikes during dual-link faults.
(3) Wrap **`esxcli network nic stats`** and **`nsxdp-cli`** netcpa stats inside Universal Forwarder scripts posting newline JSON into `index=vmware` **`sourcetype=esxi:nic:stats`**, enriching **`transport_node`** identifiers via CMDB when DNS diverges from NSX UUIDs.
(4) Tune probe **`interval`** versus ESXi **`CPU`** budgets so probes avoid fighting **`vsipfwwd`** watchdog cycles reporting **`tail_drop_count`**/**`noBuffer_count`** internally.
(5) Maintain **`FIELDALIAS`** maps from **`vmnic`**/**`pnic`** synonyms into **`nic_key`** plus CMDB-driven flags marking **`GENEVE`** **`TEP`** uplinks separately from **`mgmt`**, **`vMotion`**, or **`storage`** uplinks.

**Step 2 — SPL search and alert wiring**
Promote the **`spl`** payload into saved searches wrapped by **`macros/nsxt_pnic_fault`** once **`index`** aliases stabilize.

```spl
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_ops)
(sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:transport_node" OR sourcetype="esxi:nic:stats")
earliest=-24h@m
| eval tn_key=trim(coalesce(transport_node_id,transport_node,tn_uuid,entity_id,host,hostname,management_ip,""))
| eval nic_key=lower(trim(coalesce(vmnic,pnic,pNic,network_adapter,nic_name,if_name,"")))
| eval rx_errors_num=tonumber(coalesce(rx_errors,rx_errors_total,"0"))
| eval tx_errors_num=tonumber(coalesce(tx_errors,tx_errors_total,"0"))
| eval rx_drops_num=tonumber(coalesce(rx_drops,rx_drop_count,in_discards,"0"))
| eval tx_drops_num=tonumber(coalesce(tx_drops,tx_drop_count,out_discards,"0"))
| eval fcs_errors_num=tonumber(coalesce(fcs_errors,fcs_err,bad_fcs,"0"))
| eval crc_errors_num=tonumber(coalesce(crc_errors,crc_err,bad_crc_count,"0"))
| eval alignment_errors_num=tonumber(coalesce(alignment_errors,in_errors_align,"0"))
| eval pause_frames_rx_num=tonumber(coalesce(pause_frames_rx,rx_pause_frames,"0"))
| eval tail_drop_num=tonumber(coalesce(tail_drop_count,rx_tail_drop_count,"0"))
| eval nobuffer_num=tonumber(coalesce(noBuffer_count,no_buffer_errors,"0"))
| eval nsxt_vlan_overlay_rx_drop=tonumber(coalesce(nsxt_vlan_overlay_pnic_rx_drop,"0"))
| eval nsxt_vlan_overlay_tx_drop=tonumber(coalesce(nsxt_vlan_overlay_pnic_tx_drop,"0"))
| eval nsxt_overlay_encap_err=tonumber(coalesce(nsxt_overlay_tep_encap_error,"0"))
| eval nsxt_bfd_pnic_err=tonumber(coalesce(nsxt_bfd_pnic_error,"0"))
| eval dp_overlay_fault=nsxt_vlan_overlay_rx_drop+nsxt_vlan_overlay_tx_drop+nsxt_overlay_encap_err+nsxt_bfd_pnic_err
| eval phy_fault=fcs_errors_num+crc_errors_num+alignment_errors_num
| eval composite_fault=rx_errors_num+tx_errors_num+phy_fault+tail_drop_num+nobuffer_num
| eval overlay_pressure=rx_drops_num+tx_drops_num
| where composite_fault>0 OR overlay_pressure>50 OR pause_frames_rx_num>2500 OR dp_overlay_fault>0
| bin _time span=15m
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
    sum(rx_errors_num) as sum_rx_errors,
    sum(tx_errors_num) as sum_tx_errors,
    sum(rx_drops_num) as sum_rx_drops,
    sum(tx_drops_num) as sum_tx_drops,
    sum(composite_fault) as sum_composite_fault,
    max(fcs_errors_num) as peak_fcs,
    max(crc_errors_num) as peak_crc,
    max(alignment_errors_num) as peak_align,
    max(pause_frames_rx_num) as peak_pause_rx,
    max(tail_drop_num) as peak_tail_drop,
    max(dp_overlay_fault) as peak_dp_overlay_fault,
    max(nsxt_overlay_encap_err) as peak_encap_err
  by _time tn_key nic_key
| sort 0 tn_key nic_key _time
| streamstats window=4 global=f avg(sum_rx_errors) as prev_avg_rx_err by tn_key nic_key
| eval rx_jump=sum_rx_errors-prev_avg_rx_err
| eval overlay_pressure_sum=sum_rx_drops+sum_tx_drops
| eval severity_mark=case(
    peak_fcs>0 OR peak_crc>0 OR peak_align>0, "physical_crc_fcs_align",
    peak_pause_rx>10000, "pause_upstream_pressure",
    peak_tail_drop>100 OR rx_jump>75, "ring_tail_drop_burst",
    peak_encap_err>0 OR peak_dp_overlay_fault>0, "geneve_overlay_dp_counters",
    overlay_pressure_sum>500 AND sum_composite_fault=0, "drops_without_incremental_phy_errors",
    1=1, "general_fault_watch")
| where severity_mark!="general_fault_watch" OR rx_jump>50 OR overlay_pressure_sum>200 OR peak_pause_rx>5000
| sort -last_seen
| head 400
| table last_seen tn_key nic_key severity_mark peak_fcs peak_crc peak_align peak_pause_rx peak_tail_drop peak_encap_err peak_dp_overlay_fault sum_rx_errors sum_tx_errors sum_rx_drops sum_tx_drops rx_jump sum_composite_fault overlay_pressure_sum
```

Schedule **`cron */15 * * * *`** evaluations across **`earliest=-24h@m`** ranges and throttle **`tn_key`**/**`nic_key`** pairing four hours after triggers so cabling churn dedupes paging storms.

**Step 3 — Validate against VMware consoles**
(1) SSH to ESXi and compare **`esxcli network nic stats get -n vmnicX`** `Packets`, `Errors`, `Dropped` totals versus Splunk aggregates within ±10% skew envelopes acknowledging scripted polling jitter.
(2) Inspect **`vSphere Client`** Host ▸ Configure ▸ Networking ▸ Physical adapters panes for **`speed`**, **`duplex`**, and **`LLDP`** neighbors aligning with **`alignment_errors`** bursts tied to **`auto_negotiation_renegotiation`** hypotheses.
(3) Validate **`NSX Manager`** System ▸ Fabric ▸ Hosts ▸ Status grids so REST **`snapshots`** agree with **`transport_node`** identifiers Splunk surfaces (`entity_id`, `display_name` pairs).
(4) Compare **`nsxdp-cli netcpa stats`** CLI counters against **`peak_dp_overlay_fault`** spikes ensuring **`nsxt-overlay-tep-encap-error`** climbs precede escalation toward physical **`MTU`** **`1700`** tuning conversations.

**Step 4 — Operationalize dashboards**
• Rack versus optic heat-map layering **`rack_id`** with **`peak_crc`**/**`peak_fcs`** per **`vmnic`** highlighting scratched optics clustering suspicious TOR bundles.
• Matrix crossing **`transport_node`** with **`nic_key`** coloring **`tail_drop_num`** versus **`pause_frames_rx`** so operators separate pause floods from ring starvation.
• Top-ten leaderboard ranking **`sum_composite_fault`** density normalized against measured Gbps feeds when **`metrics`** indexes capture throughput telemetry.
• Rolling twenty-four hour **`streamstats`** slopes on **`rx_jump`** exposing creeping optic degradation ahead of `carrier` loss alarms.

**Step 5 — Tuning and extensions**
• Correlate **`severity_mark`** classes with **`UC-18.2.5`** **`GENEVE`** **`BFD`** narratives whenever tunnels remain **`UP`** while `physical` counters disagree (logical versus physical divergence guardrail).
• Blend **`alarm.NetworkConnectivityLostAlarm`** timelines with **`vmkernel`** excerpts plus **`CMDB`** **`CHG`** CSV lookups (`chg_active`) feeding **`suppress`** tables during cabling maintenance.
• Extend enrichment toward Cisco Enterprise Networking or Arista EOS syslog **`CRC`** counters referencing identical TOR **`interface`** **`IDs`** mapped via `lldp` **`neighbor`** tokens surfaced through `esxcli` discovery commands when licensed.
• Maintain **`vlan_mtu_watch`** enrichment highlighting GENEVE inner **`1450`** ceilings whenever `physical` **`MTU`** stays **`1500`**, illustrating fragmentation (inner Ethernet plus GENEVE outer header).
• Exclude **`Edge`** **`DPDK`** dataplanes intentionally (`bare-metal` **`Edge`** throughput belongs to **`UC-18.2.4`**); label dashboards TN hypervisor **`pNIC`** focus so reviewers avoid confusing Tier-0 **`SR`** throughput widgets with ESXi **`vmnic`** telemetry.
grandmaExplanation: We listen for trouble on the thick cords plugged into each rack machine where invisible tunnels ride between computers. Rising crackle there means cramped buffers or scratched optics before apps complain.
hardware: ESXi transport-node hosts (Dell/HPE/Cisco/Lenovo with Broadcom/Mellanox/Intel pNICs)
